---
title: "Codebase review — dev-readiness audit before next big push"
author: "Steve Haigh + Claude (max-effort review session)"
date: "2026-05-23"
format:
  pdf:
    include-in-header: ../pandoc-header.tex
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
---

# Context

Saturday-afternoon max-effort review of `main` branch (commit
`b33d5313`) by Claude, spawning four parallel deep-dive agents
(architecture, tests, documentation, tech debt) then synthesising.
Goal: identify what needs cleaning up before the next big push, so
that work lands on a good base.

The "next big push" is either issue #32 (kinetics-as-data refactor,
moving ~150 numerical constants into TOML / TSV files) or one of the
next biology pathways (#10 P2Y12, #33 TP, #34 GPVI). Both would
benefit from the same pre-work.

This document is the curated output. Reread Monday 2026-05-25 before
deciding what to do first.

# Verdict

**Good foundation with ~5 focused items to clear before the next big
push.** Nothing structural is wrong; a small amount of polish makes
the next month's work significantly safer.

Total clear-the-decks effort: **~1 hour of doc fixes + ~4–6 hours of
test scaffolding**. Then the path is open for either #32 or a new
biology pathway.

# What's already strong (don't change)

- **Engine/biology abstraction boundary is clean.** `CalciumDynamics`
  (process) is an ~80-line thin wrapper around `CalciumSignalling`
  (rate-law-bearing dataclass). The pure-function interface
  `molecules_to_next_time_step()` ports trivially to a different
  engine (Vivarium per issue #50).
- **Parameter provenance is thorough.** Every rate constant in
  `calcium_signalling.py` carries source citations (Dolan 2014, Purvis
  2008, Caride 2007, deYoung-Keizer 1992, …) and calibration-coupling
  flags inline. Hard to get this right; the project did.
- **Phase 3 + byte-identical regression infrastructure landed.** The
  two golden NPZ files in `models/platelet/tests/sim/golden/` are
  exactly the protection #32 needs. Without these the refactor would
  be too risky.
- **Issue triage is current.** Biology/tech labels just applied,
  28 open issues properly grouped, colleagues-share doc landed.
- **Mypy clean** on 43 source files; full platelet test suite 37/37
  green in 22 s.

# Punch list — fix before next big work

## Priority 1 — small fixes that unblock or de-risk everything else (~1 hour total)

| # | Item | Why now | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **README.md Azure URL is stale**: says `wcecoli-webapp.uksouth.azurecontainer.io`; the real deploy is `platelet-wcm.uksouth.azurecontainer.io` (CLAUDE.md is correct). | Anyone reading the README to deploy will hit the wrong endpoint. | 1 min |
| 2 | **CLAUDE.md doesn't mention the docs site.** `buildDocsSite.py` exists and works; Pages serves the index at `https://stevehaigh.github.io/platelet-wcm/reports/site/`. New readers can't discover this. | Big DX win for the small cost. | 5 min |
| 3 | **`CALIBRATION-COUPLING.md`** — a one-page matrix listing the documented "if you change X you must re-derive Y" chains (`K_SERCA` → `GAMMA_IP3R_S`; `N_IP3R` → `GAMMA_IP3R_S`; `CA_EX_UM` → SOCE/PM leak; DTS buffers → resting Ca²⁺ band). Currently each is an inline comment in `calcium_signalling.py`; nothing aggregates them. | #32 will move these constants into TOML and you'll want a single place to consult the coupling map during validation. | 15 min |
| 4 | **Design doc navigator**: 20 docs in `reports/design/` with overlapping titles, no signposting between "current canonical" (`model-status-2026-05-13`, `kinetics-as-data-2026-05-22`) and "v0.2 historical context" (`biology-overview-2026-05-07`, `demo-2026-05-07`, etc.). | Mike or any future collaborator opens that directory and has no idea which to read. | 15 min |
| 5 | **Lab-book auto-generation note** in CLAUDE.md: one sentence pointing at `runscripts/manual/buildDocsSite.py` so future-self knows the docs site index is auto-generated. | Currently invisible. | 2 min |

## Priority 2 — test scaffolding before #32 (~4–6 hours)

The byte-identical regression suite catches numerical drift, but
several modules have **zero direct unit tests** and #32 will touch
every K_* dict in the model.

| Module | LoC | Test density | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| `models/platelet/processes/resting_decay.py` | 40 | **0 direct tests** | Stochastic binomial decay; trivial to get the probability wrong; current sim-level tests don't catch off-by-ones |
| `models/platelet/listeners/calcium_trace.py` | 186 | "writes output" only | The SOCE flux + CaM ladder + PMCA sub-state calculations have no unit-level scrutiny |
| `reconstruction/platelet/dataclasses/process/calcium_signalling.py` ODE RHS | ~600 | Indirect via Phase 3 only | Phase 3 catches gross breakage but not stoichiometry errors in individual reactions |

Recommended additions, ordered by ROI:

1. **RestingDecay unit tests** (~1 h) — 100-replicate ensemble mean against
   exponential formula; edge cases (`dt=0`, `t_half=∞`, all-counts=0).
2. **CalciumTrace field calculations** (~2 h) — mock `BulkMolecules`
   with known counts, assert SOCE flux + CaM ladder counts + units
   conversion.
3. **Mark slow tests** (~30 min) — add `@pytest.mark.slow` to
   `test_phase3.py`, `test_byte_identical.py`, `test_regression.py`;
   default suite drops to ~3 s for fast local iteration; CI runs full.

## Priority 3 — decisions to make (or defer) (~30 min decision; hours-to-days if executed)

| # | Decision | Recommendation | If yes, when |
|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | **Split `calcium_signalling.py`?** 1372 LoC. Natural seams: `calcium_core` (IP3R/SERCA/PMCA/SOCE/buffers ODE), `calcium_inputs` (GPCR + agonist forcing), `calcium_parameters` (all K_* + scalars). | **Defer until #32 forces it.** Once parameters move to TOML the file shrinks dramatically (the K_* dicts are ~400 of those 1372 lines). Splitting twice — once now, once during #32 — is wasted effort. | After #32 Phase 2 |
| 6 | **Formalise the module-mutation override pattern**? Currently `runPlateletSim.py` does `cs_mod.CA_EX_UM = X` + `CalciumDynamics._*_peak_*` mutations. 9 call sites total, all in runscripts. | A `SimulationConfig` dataclass would be cleaner but the current pattern works and is well-contained. | After #32; goes naturally with the v2 webapp prep (#49). |
| 7 | **Include `wholecell/tests/` in CI**? 89 tests exist for framework-level utilities (units, struct arrays, filepaths); not run by the platelet CI workflow. | **Yes, low risk.** Adds ~5 s to CI; catches framework-level breakage that platelet tests miss. | Add to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` as a second pytest invocation. |
| 8 | **PR #48 (TUI Textual rewrite)** — ready to merge after the interactive validation you did. | **Squash-on-merge** to collapse the 15 commits into one logical change. | Whenever — no dependency on the above. |

# Things definitely NOT to do now

- **Don't migrate to Vivarium** (#50). PhD Y1 work; not during
  dissertation runway.
- **Don't fix the 47 legacy `wholecell/` TODOs.** They're from the
  wcEcoli inheritance and mostly about transcription/replication code
  that platelets don't use. Cost > benefit.
- **Don't bump mypy strictness.** Webapp legacy errors aren't in the
  platelet target paths; tightening would surface noise without
  preventing real bugs.
- **Don't refactor the legacy E. coli directory patterns in
  `wholecell/webapp/results.py`** (variant/generation/daughter
  walking). Cosmetic; not causing problems.

# Recommended order for the next ~2 weeks of dev runway

```
Day 1 (1-2 h)
  ├─ Priority-1 doc fixes (README Azure URL, CLAUDE.md docs site
  │   mention, CALIBRATION-COUPLING.md, design-doc navigator,
  │   lab-book auto-gen note)
  └─ commit and push as one "pre-#32 doc cleanup" commit

Day 2 (3-4 h)
  ├─ RestingDecay unit tests
  ├─ CalciumTrace field-calc unit tests
  ├─ @pytest.mark.slow split
  ├─ wholecell/tests/ added to CI
  └─ commit and push

Day 3 onwards
  ├─ Either: start #32 Phase 2 (parameter loader + TOML schema)
  │   — regression suite + test scaffold catch refactor breakage
  ├─ Or: pick next biology pathway (P2Y12 #10 or TP #33 — each ~1-2 weeks)
  └─ Or: PR #48 TUI merge (independent track)
```

# Cross-cutting observations

## Architecture verdict (Agent A)

**GOOD with one localized concern.** Clean separation of concerns
(engine vs biology vs orchestration), well-documented parameter
coupling, clear ODE-based biology abstraction. Main risk is scope-
limited: the 1372-line `calcium_signalling.py` is comprehensively
commented but should be split once additional pathways arrive. The
mutation-override pattern is well-contained and documented.

## Test coverage verdict (Agent B)

**ADEQUATE with critical gaps for upcoming changes.** 21.9 s total
runtime, all 37 tests passing. Byte-identical and golden-run tests
catch gross breakage. Unit tests for domain logic (RestingDecay,
CalciumTrace, ODE rate laws) are missing — risk for #32 refactoring
and new-receptor additions.

## Documentation verdict (Agent C)

**~92% accurate.** CLAUDE.md and README.md internally consistent and
faithful to the codebase. Module docstrings align with
implementations. Primary issues: stale Azure URL in README, docs site
not documented anywhere, design-doc directory navigation missing.

## Tech-debt verdict (Agent D)

**MEDIUM.** Most debt is localized. Top items: calibration-coupling
documentation gap, mypy strictness disabled, DPI magic numbers in 12
runscripts, module-mutation pattern not yet formalised, 47 legacy
`wholecell/` TODOs (mostly irrelevant).

# Calibration-coupling map (Agent D extract)

```
K_SERCA (all 10 rate constants)
  └─→ re-derive GAMMA_IP3R_S
       └─→ constrains K_IP3R (no direct param, but affects Po equilibrium)

N_IP3R
  └─→ affects IP3R resting flux
       └─→ re-derive GAMMA_IP3R_S if N_IP3R changes

CA_EX_UM (module-level, runtime override)
  └─→ gates SOCE current + PM leak (both zero if CA_EX_UM = 0)
       └─→ affects PMCA-CaM activation trajectory
       └─→ affects DTS refill rate (SERCA + SOCE)

K_CALR, K_HSP90B1_*, K_BIP (DTS luminal buffers)
  └─→ collectively shift the DTS resting Ca²⁺ level
       └─→ if changed, re-verify resting cyt = [100 nM],
                                   DTS = [250 µM] match Dolan 2014
```

This is what `CALIBRATION-COUPLING.md` (Priority 1, item 3) should
formalise.

# Action when reading this Monday

The doc is a snapshot. By Monday some of it may already be done or
stale. Quick sanity check:

1. `git log --oneline main -10` — has the doc-cleanup commit landed
   yet? If yes, skip Priority 1.
2. `PYTHONPATH=$PWD OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1 pyenv exec python -m pytest models/platelet/tests/ -q` — still 37 passing?
3. `gh issue list --state open --label biology --limit 5` — what's the
   active biology backlog look like?

Then pick Priority 1 + Priority 2 as the first 5-6 hours of work. After
that the path is clear.

# References

- Issue #32 — kinetics-as-data refactor (the major work this pre-prep
  is for); design doc `reports/design/kinetics-as-data-2026-05-22.qmd`
- Issue #49 — webapp v2 (the prep tasks pair with #32)
- Issue #50 — Vivarium migration (PhD Y1; out of scope for now)
- PR #48 — TUI Textual rewrite (independent track; ready to merge)
- `reports/lab-books/lab-book-2026-05-22-issue-44-45-dose-sweep.md`
  — most recent lab book
- `models/platelet/tests/sim/test_byte_identical.py` — the regression
  suite that makes #32 safe
